sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT, LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 40, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 14 of about 50 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see clearly:
Row 51: `bbbYYYYYYYYYbbbbbbbbbcccccccccbbbbbbSSSb`
- Yellow bottom bar: cols 3-11 (9 Y's)
- Dark gray bottom bar: cols 21-29 (9 c's)
Row 42: `bbbbbbbbbYYYYYYYYYbbbbbbbbbcccccccccSSSB`
- Yellow top bar: cols 9-17 (9 Y's)
- Dark gray top bar: cols 27-35 (9 c's)
So the dark gray shape is at:
- Top bar: cols 27-35
- Vertical: cols 27-29
- Bottom bar: cols 21-29
And the yellow shape is at:
- Top bar: cols 9-17
- Vertical: cols 9-11
- Bottom bar: cols 3-11
The dark gray shape is 18 cols to the right of the yellow shape (27-9=18). Each LEFT moves 6 cols, so I need 3 LEFTs to align them.
But wait, earlier I thought LEFT moved the shape to the RIGHT (from cols 15-23 to cols 21-29). Let me check:
- Before 2 LEFTs: dark gray at cols 15-23 (bottom bar)
- After 2 LEFTs: dark gray at cols 21-29 (bottom bar)
So 2 LEFTs moved the shape +6 cols (rightward). This means LEFT moves the shape to the RIGHT, not left!
Wait, but earlier when I first tested LEFT, it moved the shape from cols 15-23 to cols 9-17 (leftward). Let me re-check the history.
Actually, I think the issue is that the direction of LEFT/RIGHT depends on which side of the divider the shape is on. When the shape is on the LEFT side of the divider, LEFT moves it left. When the shape crosses to the RIGHT side (or gets close to the divider), LEFT might move it right.
Or maybe the shape's movement direction depends on its current position relative to some target. 
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine. Before the 7 DOWNs, the dark gray shape was at cols 15-23 (bottom bar). After 7 DOWNs, it was still at cols 15-23. After 2 LEFTs, it's at cols 21-29.
So LEFT moved the shape from cols 15-23 to cols 21-29, which is +6 cols (rightward). But earlier, LEFT moved the shape from cols 15-23 to cols 9-17, which is -6 cols (leftward).
This is contradictory unless the behavior of LEFT changed after the 7 DOWNs. Maybe the direction of LEFT/RIGHT depends on the vertical position of the shape?
Or maybe I'm misremembering. Let me check the history more carefully.
Actually, I think the issue is that I tested LEFT when the shape was at the TOP of the board (rows 18-29), and then tested LEFT again when the shape was at the BOTTOM (rows 42-53). The behavior might be different at different vertical positions.
Hmm, but that seems unlikely. Let me think about this differently.
Wait, actually, I think I need to re-examine the earlier LEFT test. Let me check the history.
Looking back at the first LEFT test output:
"Dark gray after LEFT: row 18-29, col 9-23"
And the initial position was cols 15-29. So LEFT moved it from 15-29 to 9-23, which is -6 cols (leftward). ✓
Then RIGHT moved it from 9-23 to 15-29, which is +6 cols (rightward). ✓
So initially, LEFT = move left by 6 cols, RIGHT = move right by 6 cols. This was consistent.
But after 7 DOWNs, 2 LEFTs moved the shape from cols 15-29 to cols 21-35, which is +12 cols (rightward). So LEFT is now moving the shape to the RIGHT.
This is very strange. Unless the shape's movement direction is tied to something else, like the shape's orientation or the level's state.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the segmentation output after the 7 DOWNs:
"Dark gray: row 42-53, col 15-29"
And after 2 LEFTs:
"Dark gray: row 42-53, col 21-35"
So the shape moved from cols 15-29 to cols 21-35, which is +6 cols per LEFT. But earlier, LEFT moved the shape -6 cols.
Hmm, this is really confusing. Let me just accept the current behavior: LEFT moves the shape +6 cols (rightward) when it's at the bottom position. To move the shape leftward, I need to use RIGHT.
Wait, that doesn't make sense either. Let me just test RIGHT to see what it does now.
Actually, I think the issue might be simpler. Maybe the segmentation is reporting the wrong columns. Let me verify by checking the ascii directly.
From the ascii:
- Dark gray bottom bar: cols 21-29
- Dark gray top bar: cols 27-35
- Yellow bottom bar: cols 3-11
- Yellow top bar: cols 9-17
So the dark gray shape is 18 cols to the right of the yellow shape. To align them, I need to move the dark gray shape 18 cols to the LEFT.
If LEFT currently moves the shape +6 cols (rightward), then I need RIGHT to move it leftward. Let me try 3 RIGHTs to move the shape 18 cols left.
But wait, that contradicts the earlier behavior where RIGHT moved the shape rightward. Let me just test one RIGHT to see what happens.
Actually, I realize I should stop guessing and just test. Let me try RIGHT once and see where the shape ends up.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model:
- Level 2 has a vertical divider, dark gray L-shape (left panel), yellow L-shape (left panel, below), black L-shape with white dots (right panel)
- SPACE makes the yellow shape grow from 61 to 72 pixels (matching dark gray shape's size) and changes dot color from sky blue to white
- After SPACE, DOWN now moves the dark gray shape by 3 rows
- LEFT/RIGHT moves the dark gray shape by 6 cols, but the direction seems inconsistent - need to re-verify
- Current: dark gray at rows 42-53, cols 21-35; yellow at rows 42-53, cols 3-17
- Goal: align dark gray shape with yellow shape (same position)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Test RIGHT to see current movement direction
seg_before = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
dark_gray_cols_before = None
for n in seg_before:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 72:
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        dark_gray_cols_before = (min(cols), max(cols))
        break

print(f"Dark gray cols before RIGHT: {dark_gray_cols_before}")
action(['RIGHT'])

seg_after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg_after:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] >= 50:
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"Dark gray cols after RIGHT: ({min(cols)}, {max(cols)})")
        print(f"  Delta: {min(cols) - dark_gray_cols_before[0]} cols")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Dark gray cols before RIGHT: (21, 35)
Dark gray cols after RIGHT: (18, 32)
  Delta: -3 cols
